Carelabs is a cost-effective provider of electrical arc flash analysis, investigation, and certification services for organizations . Skilled professionals will evaluate arc flash threats, personnel training, documentation, and PPE (Personal Protective Equipment) needs to ensure that your electrical safety program is current, compliant, and thorough. Carelabs can identify arc flash concerns in a timely manner and take the necessary steps to reduce the impact of harmonic distortions to ensure the equipment’s safety. c flashes are analyzed and investigated via ETAP (Electrical Transient Analysis Program) software. An arc flash is the amount of light and heat produced by an arc fault. This occurs when current travels via an air gap between two conductors.

The arc flash ionizes the surrounding air and emits a substantial amount of light and heat (approximately 35,000 degrees Fahrenheit). An arc flash analysis is the investigation and examination of a facility’s electrical systems to determine the incident energy available for each piece of electrical equipment. Arc flash studies promote safe working conditions by developing safety standards for skilled electricians working on equipment and circuits. Also included are updated one-line electrical schematics and equipment locations, and a short circuit and coordination analysis.

Arc flash studies are vital for reducing hazards and enhancing the safety of electrical staff, as well as the business’s overall safety. Studies of arc flash contribute to the determination of electrical danger levels and the installation of appropriate safety measures to reduce the risk of burns and accidents Arc flash analysis is necessary for all utilities, manufacturing, industrial, and commercial companies that use motor control, panel boards, switchboards, and electrical disconnects.
